47: **Portugal**, level on 1 point in **Group K** with **Congo DR** and positions not yet separated, open the day against **Uzbekistan**, who sit 4th in **Group K** on zero points after their opening defeat. This is the fixture **Portugal** must win emphatically to apply pressure at the top of the table — **Uzbekistan** will make it physical and compact, but the gulf in individual quality should tell if Ronaldo and company bring the urgency this group stage moment demands.
45: **England** and **Ghana** go head-to-head in the standout game of the day, with both sides level on 3 points in **Group L** and their positions not yet separated after one round each. The winner seizes clear control at the top with a matchday to spare, so the intensity will be high from the first whistle — **England** carry the edge in depth and European pedigree, but **Ghana** arrive with momentum and plenty to prove on this stage.
46: **Panama**, level on zero points in **Group L** with **Croatia** and positions not yet separated, face **Croatia**, equally unresolved at the bottom on zero points — making this a match neither side can afford to lose. With **England** and **Ghana** already ahead of both, a draw here would likely be fatal for both knockout hopes, so expect urgency and directness from two teams whose tournaments are genuinely on the line.
48: **Colombia**, top of **Group K** on 3 points, face a stiff test from **Congo DR**, who are level on 1 point in the group alongside **Portugal** with positions not yet separated. **Colombia** will be eager to build on their commanding opening and extend their lead at the summit, but **Congo DR** arrive unbeaten and with the confidence that comes from holding their ground in Matchday 1 — a point or better here would keep them firmly in contention.
43: **Argentina** and **Austria** are both level on 3 points in **Group J** — positions not yet separated after their respective opening wins — making this a match with genuine top-of-the-group stakes. **Argentina**'s superior goal difference offers a slender advantage on paper, but **Austria** arrive in form and full of confidence, and this is exactly the kind of structured, high-intensity European side that can make life difficult for even the most decorated opposition.
42: **France**, level on 3 points in **Group I** alongside **Norway** with positions not yet separated, take on **Iraq**, who sit level with **Senegal** on zero points in the group, their standing at the bottom equally unresolved. **France** carry the quality to dominate across every line, but **Iraq** need a result desperately and will defend deep and look to catch Les Bleus on the break — patience and precision will be the key for Deschamps' side.
41: **Norway**, level on 3 points in **Group I** with **France** and positions not yet separated, face a **Senegal** side also level with **Iraq** on zero points, their place in the bottom half equally unresolved. This is a must-not-lose for **Senegal**, whose tournament survival demands an immediate response, but **Norway** have looked clinical in front of goal and will back themselves to push for outright top spot with another assertive display.
44: **Jordan** and **Algeria** close out Matchday 2 in **Group J** with both sides level on zero points — each sharing the bottom half of the group with positions not yet separated — in a match that is effectively an elimination decider. Whichever side finds the drive and composure to claim three points keeps their knockout hopes alive; the other faces a near-impossible final group game to avoid an early exit.
